meaning: n.[生化]多巴,二羟基苯丙氨酸(亦作DOPA)
examples: Treatment of Parkinson's disease with L-3,4- dihydroxyphenylalanine (L-DOPA) has its limits.
The RPE cells were identified by DOPA staining and immunohistochemistry.
Tyrosinase performs action for catalytic oxidation of L-tyrosine and L-dopa to form melanin.
"DOPA is redundant and unnecessary legislation, " said Leslie Burger, president of the ALA in a statement.
The DOPA Act leaves the final definition of what counts as a social networking site to the Federal Communications Commission.
